Remember fellow $MELI baggies. Do not be fooled by stock weakness as institutions so commonly are. Having the free will to hold and accumulate a position that you have conviction in while the stock, not the business, is facing weakness is your biggest edge.

To briefly summarize what we have here is an undeniable force of a business that even during times of stress will more than likely survive and be stronger afterwards.

$MELI started as a marketplace that faced competition from $AMZN for over decade and continues to win market share and ecosystem lock-in against them.

They have recently adapted to their asset-light asian ecom competition and overall have grown into a stronger business because of the competition they have faced.

There are many developments that just started that will continue to present significant long-term value such as their full-china approach, 1p groceries, pharma, banking, off-platform advertising, full serve ad stack… the list does not seem to end and will likely keep expanding. 

All fortified by a strong culture and a management team that focuses on long-term value and startup mentality. 

Not trying to create an echo chamber here. Just reminding folks that you cannot focus on the price action. The market  can make very little sense in the short-term.

Las caídas más significativas fueron de Inter y PagBank, que retrocedieron 6 y 4 puntos respectivamente, alcanzando 69 y 66 puntos.

Hoy $MELI baja un 6%, mi opinión al respecto.

Primero aclarar que mi horizonte de inversión en esta empresa es el mediano/largo plazo. Hablo de mínimante 12/24 meses. Hay personas que me comentan que el precio de la acción baja y han pasado 10 días.

Si quieren invertir en Mercado Libre a mediano plazo, esto es para ustedes, sino busquen otras cuentas.

Hace 6 meses escribía como el mercado mira a Mercado Libre, sigo creyendo que el mercado se enfoca mucho en el corto plazo. Justamente el analista que hoy con su informe hace caer a la acción, hace 28 días le subía el target a la empresa.

Obviamente la compresión de márgenes no es positiva, todas las lupas están en Brasil, pero les doy unos datos del último trimestre de Mercado Libre en Brasil:
-GMV creció 35%.
-Artículos vendidos +45%.
-Aumento del 26% de compradores.
-Menos de 1/3 de los adultos brasileños compraron en Mercado Libre durante el 4Q 2025. MUCHO por crecer.

¿Que genera con esto? Más compradores atraen más vendedores, el ecosistema crece y arrancan las ventas cruzadas por Créditos/Mercado Pago. 

Esto se ve reflejado en los ingresos por Fintech en Brasil crecieron un 59%, siendo el aumento en "créditos" un 90%.

Obviamente puedo equivocarme, pero prefiero mirar a largo plazo. Si me dijeran que la compresión de márgenes no trae resultados lo aceptaría, pero claramente bajan márgenes y si traen resultados, la expansión del ecosistema y ventas cruzadas.

Mercado Libre cotiza actualmente a 2.9X Precio/Ventas creciendo arriba del 30%, claramente los ingresos no es lo que preocupa.

Oportunidad.

Am I right to believe that the market fundamentally misunderstands the opportunity that Mexico presents for $MELI? On the surface it looks like $AMZN's most competitive LatAm region. If you dig deeper MELI is clearly pulling away in several ways. They opened the Texas fulfillment center in 2024. This allows MELI to win in cross-border trade not only from the US but also China. The new XEM cross-docking center they opened in January is yet another competitive advantage in their regional dominance.

On the other hand MELI is quickly growing pago throughout the region at a blistering pace. With both engines accelerating in a region with such low penetration in fintech I believe we are witnessing just the beginning of MELI's dominance in Mexico.

The multiplier between Pago and ecom is truly unmatched. As they widen their marketplace selection with the Full China effort to compete with Temu at the low-end and move further upmarket against Amazon I think 2026 will show further signs that MELI is pulling away in Mexico in both ecom and fintech.

Can you ensure this is logically sound with no holes?
